message: Jake
Gordon
Miles
It's unusual that it had always been closed every time I came.
Kasbah
was open.
Even though I wasn’t a member, I decided to just go inside for now.
Oh, it’s you again.
Receptionist.
Welcome. Do you have a reservation today, or do you need something from the owner?
Uh.
Well, I guess I need something from the owner.
Receptionist.
Understood. Please wait a moment.
When the receptionist pressed the button at her desk, a tough-looking man came from the back.
Welcome.
I don't recognize your face. I am the owner of this establishment.
What may I ask is your name?
Snipes.
Mr. Snipes, I'm afraid we cannot accept new members at this time. May I kindly ask you to leave?
Receptionist.
Welcome.
Oh, it’s you.
Do you need something from the owner?
Right, can I get someone to call him?
Is it you again? This is a members-only place, and we prefer it if you don’t come here frequently.
A personal welcome from the owner? I'm flattered.
I believe I told you it would be a nuisance if you kept coming here, didn't I?
Receptionist.
Welcome.
Oh, it’s you.
Do you need something from the owner?
I see, could you call me?
Receptionist
I'm very sorry. The owner is currently out but
Got it. I'll come back later then.
I asked the receptionist, but
it seems he still hasn't returned. I'll decide to come back later.
Look
Talk
Listen
Listen
Show
Show

About Tony

This is a place where New York's prominent figures are members. It has an elegant and refined appearance.
The receptionist, with her cleavage exposed, is looking at me. It must be extremely rare for someone like me to come to this place.
Unlike ordinary nightclubs, the inside is brightly lit. Are there really no people in this bar who wish to hide their faces from others?
It's giving off a shady vibe. He’s probably a man who’s used to intimidating others and doing rough work, living in the underworld.
With a flashy red suit and sunglasses so dark you can't see through, he exudes a suspicious aura from head to toe.
With his tasteless fashion and the dubious aura he gives off, he’s a man who is utterly unsuitable for this high-class club.
At first glance, he seems like a good-natured club owner, but this guy is something else. He's a tough guy who's lived in the underworld.
Dressed in expensive clothes, with soft and clean hands that are far removed from hard labor. He gives off the vibe of a born rich.
With a face that seems kind-hearted, he’s always scheming behind the scenes to find people's weaknesses.
But you’ve really figured this place out, huh? You really live up to your reputation as a detective.
What do you mean by that?
Ah.
No, it’s nothing. Just forget it, okay?
I had no idea your office was in Chinatown. No way I could know that.
So, you’re the one who ransacked my office?
That’s not a joke. Who would go to a dump like that? I’m innocent, you know!
I don’t know anything!
Office burglar.
That's me, that's right. I confess.
I knew it.
But why would you do something like that?
I was asked by Mr. Ford to find a scalpel.
Find a scalpel, you say?
Yeah, but there wasn’t one, so I got frustrated and rummaged through the room before I left.
That's terrible.
Things got pretty rough after that.
You really should replace the key to your office with a better one. That lock...
...would let a thief in easily. Consider this my advice.
I’m just a naturally good person, so I can't do bad things.
No, actually, I didn’t come here today to become a member.
What do you mean by that?
I actually wanted to ask about the stage director, Mr. Tony Ford.
Sure, he is a customer of this store,
but what exactly do you want to know?
I'm busy with errands myself, so I'd like to keep this brief.
Did I cause you any trouble?
It’s regarding Tony Ford.
Just a bit.
I'd like you to tell me exactly what he did.
Actually, my office was ransacked.
However, it seems this was all orchestrated by Tony.
I see.
Indeed, I didn’t instruct such a thing, and he has no personal grudge against you.
I apologize for the inconvenience. However, I would like to return to work soon.
Actually, I came to this store a few weeks ago, you know, with Tony.
Oh, now that you mention it, there was a sudden reservation that day. You brought a man with you, which was quite rare.
So, that partner from that day was you, huh?
You just said you brought a man with you.
That means Tony is usually with women, right?
That’s a foolish question. Don’t you think it’s preferable to come to a serene place like this with a woman?
Wouldn’t it be better to ask him directly?
I think it’s time for me to—
A man's voice.
San.
Someone seems to be calling, excuse me for a moment.
Oh, the guy with the hat.
Isn’t he the guy who was waiting for me when I came out of Günter’s office before?
Looks like he’s noticed me too,
and as I greeted him, he approached.
The guy in the red suit.
Hey, we meet again.
—Snipes.
Who the hell are you?
I’m—
You know what? Cool name, right?
Does what he did have anything to do with Tony?
Tony is really a handful.
As an apology, I'll tell you about a shop he frequently visits.
Is that true?
It's a shop where his boyfriend hangs out. If all goes well, you should be able to find out a lot about Tony. It's called SO WHAT?, a gay club.
Gay Club
There's a boy who looks like a girl named Anju there. That was supposed to be Tony's boyfriend.
Anju's actually into people like you, so if you go, he might approach you.
Do you happen to recall the name Ginny? She's a lady who accompanies Tony.
I can't remember every customer's companion's name, you know.
I apologize, but I can't answer questions about things I truly don't know.
You'll find out if you go. It's more of a place with a lot of men.
I'm not really interested in that.
Then you should be careful with the back.
W-wait.
Just kidding. Hahaha!
I've seen Anju once, and he's really beautiful like a woman.
He works for me. If there's any trouble, please let me know right away.
I'm sorry, due to my inadequate supervision, I've caused you trouble.
No, it wasn't a big issue.
Honestly,
that guy.
Seriously, that one is a real headache. We need to educate him properly about who the boss is.
Are you close with Tony?
No, Mr. Ford is important to me. So I'll do whatever he asks.
Huff.
Yeah, that's the kind of relationship.
Yup, I’m a loyal servant.
Mr. Ford is quite something.
Why don't you cut it out,
Oh,
San
Excuse me, but,
did something happen?
What is your relationship with him?
San is someone who picked me up, a very important person. By the way, just to be clear,
it wasn't me who messed up your office, so remember that.
This guy is the real culprit behind the office mess.
A lowly detective like you shouldn't be poking your nose in this. It would be wise for you to back off before you get burned, right?
Can I take that as a threat?
Not at all! It’s just a piece of advice, as a friend.
A friend? Who became that at any point? At least, I don't remember extending a handshake!
You really are a stubborn little brat. Engaging with someone like you brings no good whatsoever!
Let’s wrap up this detective play and go home.
Wasn't this woman who came with Tony?
What do you think? This woman might have been here too,
but it’s not like I always come with the same woman.
Do you recognize
all beautiful women shine similarly. I can't distinguish them at all.
No matter how many times I see it, the answer remains the same.
Do you recognize this?
Th-That's!
Why do you have that!?!
You see, I found this at my office.
W-Wow!
Why would it be dropped in your office?
Ah, I see! When we met the other day, it must have snagged on your clothes and ended up at my office! Yes, that's it!
That can’t possibly be true.
No way, that's for sure! Anyway, thanks for delivering it!
I missed it, you know. I was feeling lonely without it.
Please give it back, it's a custom item!
If you tell me the truth, I'll give it back.
Ugh.
No way!
Please, I beg you, give it back.
I've been searching for it everywhere.
Well, it's not really my style, so I'll return this.
Oh, thank you so much!
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
